title: Why use SQL expressions for multi-source data?

blocks.content:
Transformations combine query results in the browser. They work well for simple single-column joins, but they have limits. When you need to join on multiple columns, apply WHERE filters before visualization, write conditional logic with CASE WHEN, or combine data for alert rules, you need something more powerful.

SQL expressions run on the Grafana server. They use MySQL-like syntax and treat each query's RefID as a table name. This means you can write standard SQL JOINs across data from completely different backends — Prometheus metrics joined with PostgreSQL business data, for example.

## What you'll do in this path

You'll build two TestData queries, add a SQL expression, and write a JOIN that combines their results. Along the way, you'll learn the recommended workflow for building and debugging SQL expressions, including how to handle NULL values with COALESCE.

In the next milestone, you build the base queries that your SQL expression will operate on.
